channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Opinion: Social media’s ‘big tobacco moment’ is coming
item.description: Like smoking, social media platforms have proved detrimental to children's health and can be fought back in the courts.
item.dc:creator: Parmy Olson, The Mercury News

item.title: ICYMI: the 8 biggest tech stories of the week, from new ChatGPT upgrades to Polaroid's latest instant camera
item.description: We're just about catching our breath again from the latest week in tech – and these were the biggest stories.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Stop sorting your garbage with this new technology
item.description: Recycling robots are using artificial intelligence to learn how to sort recyclables by recognizing patterns in colors, textures, shapes and logos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 ways diversity and inclusion help teams perform better
item.description: Diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) initiatives have been taking a beating lately. On his first day in office, US President Donald Trump issued an executive order calling government efforts to advance racial equity and to support underserved communities “illegal and immoral discrimination.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The project of reform and revival that saved JAL
item.description: A major turning point came for JAL in 2010 when the airline filed for bankruptcy and subsequently announced a proposed rehabilitation plan that included a much needed IT system renewal. Since 1967, JAL used the JALCOM reservation and ticketing system, built using Assembler on an IBM mainframe.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New Jersey is suing Discord for allegedly violating child safety laws
item.description: New Jersey filed a lawsuit against Discord on Thursday, alleging that the social platform recklessly exposed children to “harassment, abuse, and sexual exploitation by predators who lurk on the platform.” The move makes it the first state to sue Discord.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Google ruled an online ad monopoly, could be forced to break up its advertising products
item.description: In a landmark decision, a US District Judge has ruled that Google illegally monopolized the ad tech market.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Facebook sending out $40 payments to users over 'Like button' issue
item.description: The lawsuit claimed Facebook was using cookies to collect data on users in 2010 and 2011, even when they were logged out or visiting other websites.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: SpaceX is the top contender to build Trump’s ‘Golden Dome’ missile shield, sources say
item.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX and two partners have emerged as frontrunners to win a crucial part of President Donald Trump’s “Golden Dome” missile defense shield, six people familiar with the matter said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Netflix's first quarter builds on recent momentum as trade war drags down other tech companies
item.description: Netflix fared better than analysts anticipated during the first three months of the year, signaling the world’s largest video streaming service is still thriving as President Donald Trump’s policies cast a pall on the economy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The AI starter pack trend is taking over LinkedIn and TikTok
item.description: What’s in your office starter pack? La Colombe cold brew and a New Yorker subscription? Bose headphones and Brooks Brothers? Thanks to the latest ChatGPT trend making the rounds, you can now find out.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Latest Instagram feature asks if you’re ready to reveal your innermost Reel personality to your besties
item.description: Instagram's latest feature lets you create a custom Reels feed in your DMs that appeals to you and your besties' interests.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Tesla’s first quarter EV registrations slump 15.1% in California
item.description: Tesla‘s electric-vehicle registrations in California dropped 15.1% during the first quarter, industry data showed, signaling an accelerated decline and growing challenges for the Elon Musk-led automaker in its biggest U.S. market.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Nvidia announces plans to make AI supercomputers in US
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean full-stack AI startup Upstage eyes expansion in the US and Japan
item.description: South Korean AI startup Upstage is ramping up global expansion in the US and Japan, positioning its full-stack AI platform to serve enterprise clients.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spotify down for thousands of users
item.description: Music streaming service Spotify was down for hours on Wednesday for tens of thousands of users. The outage began around Wednesday morning, with more than 48,600 users reporting issues around the same time at 9:40 a.m. EDT, according to outage tracking site Downdetector.com.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Analysts: Go slow on M365, Google Workspace ‘agent-ish’ AI rollouts
item.description: The last few months have seen an explosion of new agentic-like AI tools that can automate workflows within productivity applications, but analysts are recommending that enterprises carefully assess deployment risks before adopting them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with more than 1,000 fearing deportation
item.description: The speed and scope of the federal government’s efforts to terminate the legal status of international students have stunned colleges and universities across the country.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Makiya Seminera And Christopher L. Keller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Accelerate Together
item.description: Use APIs to understand cloud vulnerabilities, recommends the public sector solutions engineering lead for Wiz. The post Accelerate Together — Zero Trust 2025: Wiz’s Chris Saunders on extending ZTA to cloud first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 7 reasons low-code and no-code tools fail to deliver
item.description: The potential benefits of low-code and no-code development tools include faster application development, lower expenses, and more agility. The technology is not suited to every scenario, however, and in some cases, low- and no-code solutions could be a barrier to productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 5 mobile privacy terms you need to know to protect yourself
item.description: The CyberGuy breaks down five mobile privacy terms that could make a difference when it comes to keeping your personal information safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
